_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to select the best tools to enumerate a given set of host machines in order to validate whether they match known baselines.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data and AI requirements, evaluating trade-offs between performance, maintainability, team capability, ecosystem maturity, and cost.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.418), AI-T-013 (AI, cosine: 0.416).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.499). Shared vocabulary: [ability, select, tools]. DCWF-only terms [baselines, best, enumerate, given, host, known] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, appropriate, capability, cost, data, ecosystem] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.874, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 23-word DCWF element and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Ability to select the best tools to enumerate a given set of host machines in or" and "Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.429 < STS 0.499 (gap -0.070). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [baselines, best, enumerate] and [ai, appropriate, capability] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.8%. Shared tokens: [ability, select, tools]. DCWF-only: [baselines, best, enumerate, given, host, known]. WIDAI-only: [ai, appropriate, capability, cost, data, ecosystem].
